title: Toolbar
description: Toolbar organizes actions into a compact row or column with support for grouping, toggles, split buttons, dividers, overflow, and mixed custom content.
intro: Use `ui-toolbar` for command rows, editor headers, file actions, and other compact action clusters. Keep the highest-frequency actions visible and separate unrelated groups clearly.
importCode: import { ToolbarComponent, type ToolbarItem, type ToolbarGroup } from 'ui';

featureSections.title: Basic action row
featureSections.description: A simple horizontal toolbar is enough for small command clusters such as document, file, or page actions.

featureSections.title: Grouped actions
featureSections.description: Groups add structure without forcing a separate component. They work well when clipboard, formatting, and insert actions should stay visually distinct.

featureSections.title: Size and orientation
featureSections.description: Toolbar can run horizontally or vertically. Size changes help match compact side panels or larger primary command rows.

featureSections.title: Split actions
featureSections.description: Split buttons fit actions such as save or publish where there is one primary action and a small set of related alternatives.

featureSections.title: Overflow in constrained space
featureSections.description: Overflow mode is useful when the action count is larger than the available width or when the toolbar sits inside a resizable shell.

featureSections.title: Composition with other controls
featureSections.description: A toolbar often lives beside dropdowns, search fields, or filters. This pattern is common in editor headers and dense work surfaces.

featureSections.title: Buttons, toggles, splits, dividers, and custom content
featureSections.description: The component supports mixed item types in one row. Keep the sequence purposeful and avoid turning one toolbar into a crowded control shelf.

accessibility.title: Toolbar semantics
accessibility.body: `ui-toolbar` groups related actions into one command surface. Individual controls inside the toolbar keep their own semantics, such as button, toggle button, menu button, or custom projected content.

accessibility.title: Keyboard behavior
accessibility.body:
Keyboard behavior primarily comes from the controls placed inside the toolbar.

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Tab / Shift+Tab | Move focus into, through, and out of the toolbar according to document order. |
| Enter / Space | Activate the focused button, toggle, or split action. |
| Arrow keys | Follow the behavior of the focused inner control or menu when applicable. |

accessibility.title: Labels and tooltips
accessibility.body: Icon-only actions should still expose a clear accessible name through `ariaLabel`, `label`, or tooltip-derived labeling. When you project custom content, make sure that content remains keyboard reachable and semantically clear.

apiSections.title: ToolbarComponent - ui-toolbar
apiSections.lead: Standalone command surface component for rendering toolbar items or grouped toolbar items. It supports standard buttons, toggles, dividers, split buttons, and projected custom content.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Grouped item collection. Groups are flattened with dividers between them.
Applies overflow styling for constrained layouts.

blocks.title: Outputs and models
rows: Emits when a non-divider item without its own `action` callback is activated.
{ id, label?, icon?, disabled?, selected?, type?, variant?, appearance?, action?, tooltip?, ariaLabel?, menuItems? }
Item model for button, divider, toggle, custom, and split toolbar entries.
Logical group of toolbar items rendered with separators between groups.

paragraphs: Use `groups` when the action set has natural clusters such as clipboard, formatting, or review actions.
For split items, keep the primary action obvious and limit the menu to closely related alternatives. Custom projected content should stay visually and semantically secondary to the main action row.
blocks.title: Notes
